response:
REASONING:
STEP 1 - ENTITIES:
ENTITIES: [
  Hospital,
  DepartmentType,
  Department,
  Person,
  PersonRole,
  Employee,
  PhysicianContract,
  ResidentSupervision,
  Patient,
  PatientMovement,
  Visitor,
  VisitorType,
  CompanionAuthorization,
  EmployeeDependent,
  Address,
  IdentificationCard,
  CardAssignment,
  CardStatusHistory,
  AccessEvent,
  ClockRecord,
  WorkContract,
  WorkShift,
  AvailabilityException,
  CardFailureEvent,
  ManualValidation,
  DepartmentOccupancySnapshot
]

STEP 2 - ATTRIBUTES:
ENTITY [Hospital]:
  * hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  code: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  address_text: varchar(255) (NULL)
  phone: varchar(30) (NULL)
  capacity_overall: int() (NULL)

ENTITY [DepartmentType]:
  * department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255) (NULL)
  is_clinical: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [Department]:
  * department_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  name: varchar(150) (NOT NULL)
  operational_capacity: int() (NOT NULL)
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [Person]:
  * person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  full_name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  cpf: varchar(14) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  rg: varchar(20) (NULL)
  date_of_birth: date() (NOT NULL)
  primary_phone: varchar(30) (NULL)
  secondary_phone: varchar(30) (NULL)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  updated_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PersonRole]:
  * person_role_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  role_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('VISITOR','PATIENT','PHYSICIAN','NURSE','RESIDENT_PHYSICIAN','RESEARCHER','ADMINISTRATIVE')
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULL)

ENTITY [Employee]:
  * employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  employee_code: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  hire_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  termination_date: date() (NULL)
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)
  primary_role: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('PHYSICIAN','NURSE','ADMINISTRATIVE')
  is_researcher: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)

ENTITY [PhysicianContract]:
  * physician_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('CLT','PJ','ON_CALL','DAILY')
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULL)
  weekly_hours: decimal(5,2) (NULL)

ENTITY [ResidentSupervision]:
  * resident_supervision_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +resident_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +supervisor_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULL)

ENTITY [Patient]:
  * patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  vip_flag: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  wristband_id: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  current_status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('AWAITING_CARE','IN_CARE','ADMITTED','UNDER_OBSERVATION','IN_SURGERY','DISCHARGED)
  admitted_at: datetime() (NULL)
  discharged_at: datetime() (NULL)

ENTITY [PatientMovement]:
  * patient_movement_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +from_department_id: int() (NULL)
  +to_department_id: int() (NULL)
  movement_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('ADMISSION','TRANSFER','DISCHARGE')
  movement_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  responsible_employee_id: int() (NULL)
  notes: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [VisitorType]:
  * visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [Visitor]:
  * visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  visit_purpose: varchar(255) (NULL)
  is_companion: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [CompanionAuthorization]:
  * companion_auth_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +authorizing_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  reason_category: varchar(50) (NOT NULL) -- ('MINOR','ELDERLY','PREGNANT','LABOR','POSTPARTUM','PROLONGED_HOSPITALIZATION','VULNERABILITY','OTHER')
  details: varchar(255) (NULL)
  valid_from: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  valid_to: datetime() (NULL)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [EmployeeDependent]:
  * employee_dependent_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  relationship: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [Address]:
  * address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  street: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  number: varchar(20) (NULL)
  complement: varchar(100) (NULL)
  neighborhood: varchar(100) (NULL)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  postal_code: varchar(15) (NULL)
  country: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Brazil')
  is_primary: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  last_update_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [IdentificationCard]:
  * card_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT','TEMPORARY')
  access_level: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)
  issue_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  expiry_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  is_personalized: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  photo_url: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [CardAssignment]:
  * card_assignment_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  assignment_start: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  assignment_end: datetime() (NULL)
  assignment_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT')
  created_by_employee_id: int() (NULL)

ENTITY [CardStatusHistory]:
  * card_status_history_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('ACTIVE','REPLACED','LOST','EXPIRED','INOPERABLE')
  status_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  reason: varchar(255) (NULL)
  related_person_id: int() (NULL)
  created_by_employee_id: int() (NULL)

ENTITY [AccessEvent]:
  * access_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +card_id: int() (NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_id: int() (NULL)
  event_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  direction: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- ('ENTRY','EXIT')
  person_category: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT','OTHER')
  is_allowed: boolean() (NOT NULL)
  reason_code: varchar(50) (NULL) -- ('NORMAL','OUT_OF_HOURS','BLOCKED_LOST_CARD','MANUAL_OVERRIDE',...)
  source_system: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('TURNSTILE','RECEPTION','SECURITY','SYSTEM')
  notes: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [ClockRecord]:
  * clock_record_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +card_id: int() (NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  clock_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  direction: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- ('IN','OUT')
  source: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('CARD','MANUAL')
  is_anomalous: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  anomaly_type: varchar(50) (NULL) -- ('MISSING_PAIR','OVERTIME','OUT_OF_SCHEDULE')
  notes: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [WorkContract]:
  * work_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ('CLT','PJ','TEMPORARY','INTERNSHIP','OTHER')
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULL)
  standard_weekly_hours: decimal(5,2) (NULL)

ENTITY [WorkShift]:
  * work_shift_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +work_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  shift_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('FIXED','ROTATING','FLEXIBLE')
  weekday: int() (NULL) -- 0-6 if applicable
  start_time: time() (NULL)
  end_time: time() (NULL)
  valid_from: date() (NOT NULL)
  valid_to: date() (NULL)
  description: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [AvailabilityException]:
  * availability_exception_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  exception_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('DAY_OFF','MEDICAL_LEAVE','VACATION','TRAINING','OTHER')
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  note: varchar(255) (NULL)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [CardFailureEvent]:
  * card_failure_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_id: int() (NULL)
  attempt_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  failure_reason: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  reader_id: varchar(50) (NULL)
  notes: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [ManualValidation]:
  * manual_validation_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_failure_event_id: int() (NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +validator_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  validation_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  validation_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- ('ENTRY','EXIT')
  official_document_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)
  official_document_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)
  is_accepted: boolean() (NOT NULL)
  notes: varchar(255) (NULL)

ENTITY [DepartmentOccupancySnapshot]:
  * occupancy_snapshot_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +department_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  snapshot_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  current_occupancy: int() (NOT NULL)
  capacity: int() (NOT NULL)
  occupancy_rate: decimal(5,2) (NOT NULL)
  alert_level: varchar(10) (NULL) -- ('60','80','100',NULL)

STEP 3 - RELATIONSHIPS:
RELATIONSHIPS:
  - Hospital relates to Department because each hospital contains multiple departments.
  - DepartmentType relates to Department because each department is of a defined type (Emergency, ICU, etc.).
  - Person relates to PersonRole because a person can have multiple roles (visitor, patient, physician, etc.).
  - Person relates to Employee because every employee is a person with additional employment data.
  - Employee relates to PhysicianContract because physicians may work at multiple units with different contract types.
  - Employee (resident) relates to Employee (supervisor) via ResidentSupervision to track supervising physicians for residents at a hospital.
  - Person relates to Patient because a patient is a person with clinical admission attributes.
  - Patient relates to PatientMovement because patient movements between departments must be fully recorded.
  - Department relates to PatientMovement because movements are between departments.
  - Person relates to Visitor because a visitor is a person with visitor-specific attributes.
  - VisitorType relates to Visitor because visitor classification determines access restrictions.
  - Patient relates to Visitor via CompanionAuthorization because some visitors are registered companions for specific patients.
  - Employee relates to CompanionAuthorization because an employee authorizes the companion.
  - Hospital relates to CompanionAuthorization because authorization is specific to a hospital.
  - Employee relates to Patient via EmployeeDependent because employees may have dependents who are also patients.
  - Person relates to Address because a person may have more than one registered address.
  - IdentificationCard relates to CardAssignment because a card may be temporarily linked to a person while in use.
  - Person relates to CardAssignment because card usage is associated with a person.
  - IdentificationCard relates to CardStatusHistory because card history (active, replaced, lost, expired, inoperable) must be maintained.
  - Person relates to CardStatusHistory optionally to show which person is involved in a status change (e.g., lost card).
  - Person relates to AccessEvent because all entries and exits are tracked per person.
  - IdentificationCard relates to AccessEvent because access attempts use cards when available.
  - Hospital relates to AccessEvent because access occurs at specific units.
  - Department relates to AccessEvent because access can be tracked per department.
  - Employee relates to ClockRecord because time & attendance records are per employee.
  - IdentificationCard relates to ClockRecord because employees use cards to clock in/out.
  - Hospital relates to ClockRecord because time records are tied to a specific unit.
  - Employee relates to WorkContract because different hospitals may maintain different contracts with the same employee.
  - Hospital relates to WorkContract because contracts are per hospital.
  - WorkContract relates to WorkShift because shifts define scheduling under a contract.
  - Employee relates to AvailabilityException because availability exceptions remove professionals from scheduled activities.
  - Hospital relates to AvailabilityException because exceptions may be specific to a hospital.
  - IdentificationCard relates to CardFailureEvent because card failures are recorded per card.
  - Person relates to CardFailureEvent because the person involved must be recorded.
  - Hospital relates to CardFailureEvent because failures happen in specific units.
  - Department relates to CardFailureEvent optionally because failures can be tied to a department entry point.
  - CardFailureEvent relates to ManualValidation because manual entry/exit validation is performed after card failure.
  - Person relates to ManualValidation because the validated person must be stored.
  - Employee relates to ManualValidation because a staff member performs manual verification.
  - Hospital relates to ManualValidation because validation occurs at a unit.
  - Department relates to DepartmentOccupancySnapshot because occupancy is measured per department.
  
STEP 4 - CARDINALITIES & FK:
CARDINALITY & FK:
  Relationship 1: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* Department:hospital_id
  Relationship 2: DepartmentType:department_type_id 1--* Department:department_type_id
  Relationship 3: Person:person_id 1--* PersonRole:person_id

  Relationship 43: CardFailureEvent:card_failure_event_id ?--* ManualValidation:card_failure_event_id

STEP 5 - VALIDATION:
VALIDATION:
  ✓ All primary keys defined and unique
  ✓ All foreign keys valid and reference existing entities
  ✓ Cardinalities are logical for the hospital network domain (persons can have multiple roles; hospitals have many departments; many-to-many relationships like patient-companion and employee-dependent are resolved via junction tables)
  ✓ Many-to-many handled with junction tables (CompanionAuthorization, EmployeeDependent, ResidentSupervision, CardAssignment)
  ✓ No orphaned tables or attributes; each table has a clear purpose tied to requirements (access control, movements, scheduling, card lifecycle, exceptions)
  ✓ Requirements covered:
      - Multiple hospitals and departments with capacities and occupancy snapshots for 60/80/100% alerts
      - People management with multiple roles, employees, patients, visitors, residents, researchers
      - VIP patients, dependents, companions and authorizations
      - Full patient movement history and current status
      - Visitor types and access restrictions
      - Multiple addresses with last update timestamps
      - Identification cards, assignment, status history, lost/inoperable handling
      - Integrated time & attendance, contracts, shifts, clock records, availability exceptions
      - Card failures and manual validations with legal validity of entries/exits
      - Access history queries by person, date, department, and type
  No issues found.

formatted_human_prompt:
Analyze the following database requirement. You MUST follow the 5-step reasoning process 
defined in the system prompt. Show your complete thinking for each step, then output the JSON.

Database requirement:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor everyone who enters and leaves the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution’s internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital contains departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network must offer every service: each hospital declares which departments, human resources and capacities it provides (available teams and departments).
Departments in the network include:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1. Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader/responsible person for every shift (i.e., there is always someone accountable for the department at any time).
2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals from multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. A single person may assume more than one role (for example, a physician may be admitted as a patient outside working hours).
* Physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types (e.g., CLT: permanent employment, PJ: contracted as a legal entity, on-call/shift, daily rate).
* Residents are licensed physicians in training who attend to patients under supervision; it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident.
* VIP patients have special rules for care and enhanced privacy (these rules relate to privacy and procedures, not to the medical quality of care).
* Employees may have dependents who are also patients.
* The hospital records the following for every person who accesses its facilities: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF = Brazilian taxpayer ID; RG = identity card.)
2.1. Patients
* Each patient receives an identification wristband.
* The system must record patient movements between departments, including admission, inter-department transfers and discharge.
* A complete history of the departments visited by each patient must be maintained.
* The system must indicate the patient’s current status: awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2. Visitors
* Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
* The type of visitor must be recorded (e.g., technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
* Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
* Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
* Not all patients are allowed to have an accompanying person. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant people; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when deemed necessary by a physician or social services.
* The system must record the visitor’s exit (mandatory).
* Access outside visiting hours must be blocked unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3. Employees
* Employees must be clearly identified.
* A researcher can only be a nurse or a physician (i.e., researcher is not an independent occupational category separate from clinical roles).
* Still, any employee may be treated by the hospital, act as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4. Addresses
* A person may have more than one registered address.
* Employees periodically update their addresses.
* When possible, patients and visitors should also have their addresses updated.
* It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
* If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should issue an alert requesting a registration update.
2.5. Identification Cards
* Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
* Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
* When a card is in use it is marked active.
* Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card’s identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person’s record while in use.
* Only personalized cards (employees) include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
The hospital units require an integrated time & attendance system covering all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or being assigned impractical schedules.
* Professionals follow different work routines.
* Administration must precisely track the hours worked by each employee.
* Different hospitals in the network may maintain different scheduling contracts with the same employee.
* It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duty, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1. Time & Attendance Records
* The system must record each employee’s clock-in and clock-out times.
* As noted, every employee has an identification card, which facilitates time recording.
* Time records should generate alerts if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record (useful for HR).
3.2. Work Shifts
* Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
* Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
* Nurses may follow fixed schedules or rotate through shifts.
* Physicians may have variable schedules and may visit the hospital only to perform specific duties (consultations, monitoring, surgeries) and leave when those tasks are complete.
* The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
* The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance.
3.3. Availability Exceptions
* Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
* Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1. Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. Upon reporting a loss:
* the card is automatically invalidated;
* a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
* every attempted access with the lost card is blocked and logged;
* the event generates an alert for security.
A card history (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2. Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record:
   * the person involved;
   * reason for the failure;
   * the read attempt;
   * manual verification performed (security/reception staff responsible).
Manual entry/exit validation using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked as inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

Remember:
1. Complete STEP 1 - STEP 5 fully before generating JSON
2. Be explicit and thorough in your reasoning
3. Reference the requirement directly when explaining your choices
4. Output ONLY the JSON after your reasoning (no additional commentary)

format_params_used.input_requirements:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor everyone who enters and leaves the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution’s internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital contains departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network must offer every service: each hospital declares which departments, human resources and capacities it provides (available teams and departments).
Departments in the network include:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1. Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader/responsible person for every shift (i.e., there is always someone accountable for the department at any time).
2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals from multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. A single person may assume more than one role (for example, a physician may be admitted as a patient outside working hours).
* Physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types (e.g., CLT: permanent employment, PJ: contracted as a legal entity, on-call/shift, daily rate).
* Residents are licensed physicians in training who attend to patients under supervision; it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident.
* VIP patients have special rules for care and enhanced privacy (these rules relate to privacy and procedures, not to the medical quality of care).
* Employees may have dependents who are also patients.
* The hospital records the following for every person who accesses its facilities: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF = Brazilian taxpayer ID; RG = identity card.)
2.1. Patients
* Each patient receives an identification wristband.
* The system must record patient movements between departments, including admission, inter-department transfers and discharge.
* A complete history of the departments visited by each patient must be maintained.
* The system must indicate the patient’s current status: awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2. Visitors
* Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
* The type of visitor must be recorded (e.g., technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
* Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
* Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
* Not all patients are allowed to have an accompanying person. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant people; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when deemed necessary by a physician or social services.
* The system must record the visitor’s exit (mandatory).
* Access outside visiting hours must be blocked unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3. Employees
* Employees must be clearly identified.
* A researcher can only be a nurse or a physician (i.e., researcher is not an independent occupational category separate from clinical roles).
* Still, any employee may be treated by the hospital, act as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4. Addresses
* A person may have more than one registered address.
* Employees periodically update their addresses.
* When possible, patients and visitors should also have their addresses updated.
* It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
* If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should issue an alert requesting a registration update.
2.5. Identification Cards
* Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
* Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
* When a card is in use it is marked active.
* Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card’s identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person’s record while in use.
* Only personalized cards (employees) include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
The hospital units require an integrated time & attendance system covering all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or being assigned impractical schedules.
* Professionals follow different work routines.
* Administration must precisely track the hours worked by each employee.
* Different hospitals in the network may maintain different scheduling contracts with the same employee.
* It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duty, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1. Time & Attendance Records
* The system must record each employee’s clock-in and clock-out times.
* As noted, every employee has an identification card, which facilitates time recording.
* Time records should generate alerts if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record (useful for HR).
3.2. Work Shifts
* Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
* Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
* Nurses may follow fixed schedules or rotate through shifts.
* Physicians may have variable schedules and may visit the hospital only to perform specific duties (consultations, monitoring, surgeries) and leave when those tasks are complete.
* The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
* The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance.
3.3. Availability Exceptions
* Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
* Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1. Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. Upon reporting a loss:
* the card is automatically invalidated;
* a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
* every attempted access with the lost card is blocked and logged;
* the event generates an alert for security.
A card history (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2. Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record:
   * the person involved;
   * reason for the failure;
   * the read attempt;
   * manual verification performed (security/reception staff responsible).
Manual entry/exit validation using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked as inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

response:
REASONING:
STEP 1 - ENTITIES:
ENTITIES: [
  Hospital,
  Department,
  HospitalDepartment,
  Person,
  PersonRole,
  Employee,
  Physician,
  Nurse,
  Resident,
  ResearcherRole,
  Patient,
  VIPPatient,
  Visitor,
  EmployeeDependent,
  Address,
  PersonAddress,
  IdentificationCard,
  CardAssignment,
  CardStatusHistory,
  CardFailureEvent,
  ManualAccessValidation,
  DepartmentShift,
  DepartmentShiftLeader,
  PatientVisit,
  PatientMovement,
  VisitorVisit,
  VisitorCompanion,
  EmployeeContract,
  WorkShiftPattern,
  EmployeeSchedule,
  AvailabilityException,
  TimeRecord,
  AccessEvent,
  DepartmentOccupancySnapshot
]

Explanation:
- Hospital network with multiple facilities → Hospital.
- Each hospital has departments, but not all hospitals have all departments; departments are standard list and then “declared” per hospital → Department + HospitalDepartment (availability/capacity per hospital).
- People of various categories with shared attributes → Person.
- One person can be visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher, administrative staff → model as roles: PersonRole plus specialized tables where needed (Employee, Physician, Nurse, Resident, ResearcherRole, Patient, Visitor).
- Employees are a subset of persons (physician, nurse, admin, etc.) → Employee.
- Physician, Nurse, Resident have specific attributes/relations (resident-supervisor) → separate subtype tables: Physician, Nurse, Resident; Researcher is a flag/role for physician/nurse only → ResearcherRole.
- VIP patients need flag/rules → can be attribute on Patient or separate; choose separate VIPPatient to keep explicit; but could be attribute. I’ll keep VIPPatient as small subtype.
- Employees may have dependents who are also patients → EmployeeDependent linking Employee to Patient.
- Addresses: multiple per person + last update timestamp → Address + PersonAddress (associative with last_update_ts).
- Identification cards: with type, issue/expiry, active flag, photo for employees only, card can be temporarily linked to person (patients/visitors), card status history (active, replaced, lost, expired) → IdentificationCard + CardAssignment (link to person over time) + CardStatusHistory.
- Card failure events → CardFailureEvent.
- Manual entry/exit validation events with official document etc. → ManualAccessValidation.
- Departments have assigned coordinator and shift leaders per time → DepartmentShift (time windows per hospital-department) + DepartmentShiftLeader (who is leader for a given shift). Coordinator could be attribute on HospitalDepartment.
- Patients: wristband, movements between departments, current status, complete history → PatientVisit (episode/admission) + PatientMovement (per movement between departments).
- Visitors: type/classification, identification card, exit recorded, access rules, can be companions to allowed patients → VisitorVisit (entry/exit per visit) + VisitorCompanion (link visit to patient as companion).
- Employee scheduling and contracts per hospital, different contract types, schedules, exceptions, time & attendance → EmployeeContract (per hospital, contract_type) + WorkShiftPattern (generic pattern templates) + EmployeeSchedule (link contract to pattern or ad-hoc schedule) + AvailabilityException.
- Time & Attendance: clock-in/out via card, alerts for missing/exceeding → TimeRecord (per event).
- Access history by person, date, department and type; access events including blocked attempts (lost cards, outside hours) → AccessEvent (generic access log for any person/card).
- Department occupancy and capacity thresholds; alerts at 60/80/100% → HospitalDepartment has capacity fields; DepartmentOccupancySnapshot holds occupancy measurements over time.

STEP 2 - ATTRIBUTES:
ENTITY [Hospital]:
  * hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  code: varchar(20) (UNIQUE)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  address_text: varchar(255) (NOT NULL)
  phone: varchar(30)
  is_active: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Department]:
  * department_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [HospitalDepartment]:
  * hospital_department_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  coordinator_person_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to Person; must be employee in business logic
  operational_capacity_beds: int() (NULLABLE)
  operational_capacity_people: int() (NULLABLE)
  is_active: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Person]:
  * person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  full_name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  cpf: varchar(14) (UNIQUE)  -- formatted CPF
  rg: varchar(20)
  date_of_birth: date() (NOT NULL)
  primary_phone: varchar(20)
  secondary_phone: varchar(20)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  updated_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PersonRole]:
  * person_role_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  role_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- ENUM-like: 'VISITOR','PATIENT','PHYSICIAN','NURSE','RESIDENT','RESEARCHER','ADMIN'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [Employee]:
  * employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  employee_number: varchar(30) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  hire_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  termination_date: date() (NULLABLE)
  main_function: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'PHYSICIAN','NURSE','ADMIN'
  is_active: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Physician]:
  * physician_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  crm_number: varchar(30) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)  -- Brazilian medical license
  specialty: varchar(100)

ENTITY [Nurse]:
  * nurse_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  coren_number: varchar(30) (UNIQUE)  -- Brazilian nursing council
  level: varchar(50)  -- e.g., 'RN','Technician'

ENTITY [Resident]:
  * resident_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +physician_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +supervisor_physician_id: int() (NOT NULL)  -- FK to Physician
  residency_program: varchar(150)
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [ResearcherRole]:
  * researcher_role_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  research_area: varchar(150)
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [Patient]:
  * patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  wristband_code: varchar(50) (UNIQUE, NOT NULL)
  current_status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- ENUM: 'AWAITING','IN_CARE','ADMITTED','OBSERVATION','SURGERY','DISCHARGED'
  vip_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  primary_physician_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to Physician
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [VIPPatient]:
  * vip_patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  vip_notes: varchar(255)
  privacy_level: varchar(50)  -- e.g., 'HIGH','MEDIUM'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [Visitor]:
  * visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  visitor_type: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'COMPANION','TECHNICAL','REGULATORY'
  classification_notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [EmployeeDependent]:
  * employee_dependent_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  relationship: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'SPOUSE','CHILD'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [Address]:
  * address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  street: varchar(150) (NOT NULL)
  number: varchar(20)
  complement: varchar(100)
  neighborhood: varchar(100)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  postal_code: varchar(15)

ENTITY [PersonAddress]:
  * person_address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +address_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  is_primary: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  last_update_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [IdentificationCard]:
  * card_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT'
  access_level: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- maps to colors/permissions
  issue_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  expiry_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  is_active: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  is_personalized: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  photo_url: varchar(255)  -- only for employees; NULL otherwise

ENTITY [CardAssignment]:
  * card_assignment_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  assignment_start_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  assignment_end_ts: datetime() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [CardStatusHistory]:
  * card_status_history_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- 'ACTIVE','REPLACED','LOST','EXPIRED','INOPERABLE'
  status_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  reason: varchar(255)
  related_person_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to Person who reported or used
  related_event_type: varchar(50)  -- e.g., 'LOSS_REPORT','REPLACEMENT'

ENTITY [CardFailureEvent]:
  * card_failure_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  person_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- person involved, if identifiable
  failure_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  failure_reason: varchar(255) (NOT NULL)
  read_attempt_location: varchar(100)  -- door/turnstile/department
  security_staff_person_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to Person
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [ManualAccessValidation]:
  * manual_validation_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  validation_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  validation_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)  -- 'ENTRY','EXIT'
  official_document_type: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)
  official_document_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)
  employee_registration_checked: varchar(50)
  validated_by_person_id: int() (NOT NULL)  -- security/reception staff (Person)
  related_card_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [DepartmentShift]:
  * department_shift_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_department_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  shift_name: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'MORNING','AFTERNOON','NIGHT'
  shift_start_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  shift_end_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  valid_from_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  valid_to_date: date() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [DepartmentShiftLeader]:
  * department_shift_leader_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +department_shift_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  leader_start_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  leader_end_ts: datetime() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [PatientVisit]:
  * patient_visit_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  admission_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  discharge_ts: datetime() (NULLABLE)
  admission_type: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)  -- e.g., 'EMERGENCY','ELECTIVE'
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [PatientMovement]:
  * patient_movement_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_visit_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  from_hospital_department_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- NULL for initial admission
  to_hospital_department_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  movement_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  movement_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- 'ADMISSION','TRANSFER','DISCHARGE'
  recorded_by_employee_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [VisitorVisit]:
  * visitor_visit_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  entry_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  exit_ts: datetime() (NULLABLE)  -- must be filled; business rule to enforce
  authorized_out_of_hours: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  authorization_person_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to Person (physician/social services)
  purpose: varchar(255)

ENTITY [VisitorCompanion]:
  * visitor_companion_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +visitor_visit_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +patient_visit_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  allowed_reason: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)  -- 'MINOR','ELDERLY','PREGNANT','LABOR','POSTPARTUM','PROLONGED','VULNERABILITY'
  authorized_by_person_id: int() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [EmployeeContract]:
  * employee_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)  -- 'CLT','PJ','ON_CALL','DAILY'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NULLABLE)
  weekly_hours: decimal(5,2) (NULLABLE)
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [WorkShiftPattern]:
  * work_shift_pattern_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255)
  default_start_time: time() (NULLABLE)
  default_end_time: time() (NULLABLE)
  weekdays_mask: varchar(7) (NULLABLE)  -- e.g., '1111100' for Mon-Fri

ENTITY [EmployeeSchedule]:
  * employee_schedule_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +work_shift_pattern_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  schedule_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  planned_start_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  planned_end_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  location_hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  location_hospital_department_id: int() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [AvailabilityException]:
  * availability_exception_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  exception_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- 'DAY_OFF','MEDICAL_LEAVE','VACATION','TRAINING'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  note: varchar(255)

ENTITY [TimeRecord]:
  * time_record_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  record_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  record_type: varchar(10) (NOT NULL)  -- 'IN','OUT'
  source: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)  -- 'CARD_READER','MANUAL'
  related_schedule_id: int() (NULLABLE)  -- FK to EmployeeSchedule
  is_missing_pair_alert: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  is_overtime_alert: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)

ENTITY [AccessEvent]:
  * access_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  person_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  access_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  access_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)  -- 'ENTRY','EXIT','ATTEMPT'
  result: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)  -- 'GRANTED','DENIED'
  denial_reason: varchar(100)
  hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  hospital_department_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  person_category: varchar(20) (NULLABLE)  -- 'EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT'
  related_card_status_history_id: int() (NULLABLE)
  related_manual_validation_id: int() (NULLABLE)

ENTITY [DepartmentOccupancySnapshot]:
  * occupancy_snapshot_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_department_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  snapshot_ts: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  current_occupancy_count: int() (NOT NULL)
  capacity_reference: int() (NOT NULL)
  threshold_level: varchar(10)  -- '60','80','100' or NULL
  generated_alert: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)

STEP 3 - RELATIONSHIPS:
RELATIONSHIPS:
  - Hospital has many HospitalDepartments because each facility declares which departments it offers and their capacities.
  - Department has many HospitalDepartments because each standard department (e.g., ICU) can exist in multiple hospitals.
  - HospitalDepartment has one coordinator (Person) and many DepartmentShifts; each shift has many DepartmentShiftLeaders over time.
  - Person can have many PersonRoles because one person may be employee, patient, visitor, etc., possibly over different periods.
  - Person has at most one Employee, Patient, Visitor record each (subtypes), via 1-1 relationships.
  - Employee specializes into Physician or Nurse (or neither) and may have a ResearcherRole; Resident ties to Physician and supervisor Physician.
  - Employee can have many EmployeeContracts across hospitals; each contract can have many EmployeeSchedules and AvailabilityExceptions.
  - Employee can have many EmployeeDependents; each dependent is a Patient.
  - Person can have many PersonAddress records; each PersonAddress links to an Address; Address can be shared by many persons.
  - IdentificationCard can be assigned to many Persons over time via CardAssignment; a person can have multiple cards over their history.
  - IdentificationCard has many CardStatusHistory entries; these capture active, replaced, lost, expired, inoperable states.
  - CardFailureEvent references a Card and optionally the involved Person and security staff (Person).
  - ManualAccessValidation references the validated Person, the validating staff Person, and optionally a Card.
  - Patient has many PatientVisits; each PatientVisit belongs to one Hospital and has many PatientMovements (admission, transfers, discharge).
  - PatientMovement references from/to HospitalDepartments and optionally the Employee who recorded it.
  - Visitor has many VisitorVisits; each VisitorVisit belongs to a Hospital and may be linked as a companion to one PatientVisit via VisitorCompanion.
  - VisitorCompanion links VisitorVisit to PatientVisit and optionally to the Person who authorized it.
  - Employee has many TimeRecords; each TimeRecord uses a Card and may relate to an EmployeeSchedule.
  - EmployeeSchedule belongs to an EmployeeContract and references the location Hospital and optional HospitalDepartment.
  - AvailabilityException belongs to an EmployeeContract.
  - AccessEvent logs use of cards/persons at Hospitals and optional HospitalDepartments; may reference CardStatusHistory (e.g., blocked lost card) and ManualAccessValidation.
  - DepartmentOccupancySnapshot belongs to HospitalDepartment to report occupancy and threshold alerts.
  - VIPPatient is a 1-1 extension of Patient to capture VIP-related data.

STEP 4 - CARDINALITIES & FK:
CARDINALITY & FK:
  Relationship 1: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* HospitalDepartment:hospital_id
  Relationship 2: Department:department_id 1--* HospitalDepartment:department_id
  Relationship 3: Person:person_id 1--* PersonRole:person_id
  Relationship 4: Person:person_id 1--? Employee:person_id
  Relationship 5: Person:person_id 1--? Patient:person_id
  Relationship 6: Person:person_id 1--? Visitor:person_id
  Relationship 7: Employee:employee_id 1--? Physician:employee_id
  Relationship 8: Employee:employee_id 1--? Nurse:employee_id
  Relationship 9: Employee:employee_id 1--? ResearcherRole:employee_id
  Relationship 10: Physician:physician_id 1--? Resident:physician_id
  Relationship 11: Physician:physician_id 1--* Resident:supervisor_physician_id
  Relationship 12: Patient:patient_id 1--? VIPPatient:patient_id
  Relationship 13: Employee:employee_id 1--* EmployeeDependent:employee_id
  Relationship 14: Patient:patient_id 1--* EmployeeDependent:patient_id
  Relationship 15: Person:person_id 1--* PersonAddress:person_id
