formatted_human_prompt:
Analyze the following database requirement. You MUST follow the 5-step reasoning process 
defined in the system prompt. Show your complete thinking for each step, then output the JSON.

Database requirement:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor everyone who enters and leaves the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution’s internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital contains departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network must offer every service: each hospital declares which departments, human resources and capacities it provides (available teams and departments).
Departments in the network include:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1. Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader/responsible person for every shift (i.e., there is always someone accountable for the department at any time).
2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals from multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. A single person may assume more than one role (for example, a physician may be admitted as a patient outside working hours).
* Physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types (e.g., CLT: permanent employment, PJ: contracted as a legal entity, on-call/shift, daily rate).
* Residents are licensed physicians in training who attend to patients under supervision; it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident.
* VIP patients have special rules for care and enhanced privacy (these rules relate to privacy and procedures, not to the medical quality of care).
* Employees may have dependents who are also patients.
* The hospital records the following for every person who accesses its facilities: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF = Brazilian taxpayer ID; RG = identity card.)
2.1. Patients
* Each patient receives an identification wristband.
* The system must record patient movements between departments, including admission, inter-department transfers and discharge.
* A complete history of the departments visited by each patient must be maintained.
* The system must indicate the patient’s current status: awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2. Visitors
* Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
* The type of visitor must be recorded (e.g., technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
* Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
* Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
* Not all patients are allowed to have an accompanying person. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant people; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when deemed necessary by a physician or social services.
* The system must record the visitor’s exit (mandatory).
* Access outside visiting hours must be blocked unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3. Employees
* Employees must be clearly identified.
* A researcher can only be a nurse or a physician (i.e., researcher is not an independent occupational category separate from clinical roles).
* Still, any employee may be treated by the hospital, act as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4. Addresses
* A person may have more than one registered address.
* Employees periodically update their addresses.
* When possible, patients and visitors should also have their addresses updated.
* It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
* If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should issue an alert requesting a registration update.
2.5. Identification Cards
* Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
* Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
* When a card is in use it is marked active.
* Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card’s identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person’s record while in use.
* Only personalized cards (employees) include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
The hospital units require an integrated time & attendance system covering all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or being assigned impractical schedules.
* Professionals follow different work routines.
* Administration must precisely track the hours worked by each employee.
* Different hospitals in the network may maintain different scheduling contracts with the same employee.
* It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duty, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1. Time & Attendance Records
* The system must record each employee’s clock-in and clock-out times.
* As noted, every employee has an identification card, which facilitates time recording.
* Time records should generate alerts if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record (useful for HR).
3.2. Work Shifts
* Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
* Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
* Nurses may follow fixed schedules or rotate through shifts.
* Physicians may have variable schedules and may visit the hospital only to perform specific duties (consultations, monitoring, surgeries) and leave when those tasks are complete.
* The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
* The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance.
3.3. Availability Exceptions
* Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
* Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1. Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. Upon reporting a loss:
* the card is automatically invalidated;
* a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
* every attempted access with the lost card is blocked and logged;
* the event generates an alert for security.
A card history (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2. Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record:
   * the person involved;
   * reason for the failure;
   * the read attempt;
   * manual verification performed (security/reception staff responsible).
Manual entry/exit validation using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked as inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

Remember:
1. Complete STEP 1 - STEP 5 fully before generating JSON
2. Be explicit and thorough in your reasoning
3. Reference the requirement directly when explaining your choices
4. Output ONLY the JSON after your reasoning (no additional commentary)

format_params_used.input_requirements:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor everyone who enters and leaves the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution’s internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital contains departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network must offer every service: each hospital declares which departments, human resources and capacities it provides (available teams and departments).
Departments in the network include:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1. Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader/responsible person for every shift (i.e., there is always someone accountable for the department at any time).
2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals from multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. A single person may assume more than one role (for example, a physician may be admitted as a patient outside working hours).
* Physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types (e.g., CLT: permanent employment, PJ: contracted as a legal entity, on-call/shift, daily rate).
* Residents are licensed physicians in training who attend to patients under supervision; it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident.
* VIP patients have special rules for care and enhanced privacy (these rules relate to privacy and procedures, not to the medical quality of care).
* Employees may have dependents who are also patients.
* The hospital records the following for every person who accesses its facilities: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF = Brazilian taxpayer ID; RG = identity card.)
2.1. Patients
* Each patient receives an identification wristband.
* The system must record patient movements between departments, including admission, inter-department transfers and discharge.
* A complete history of the departments visited by each patient must be maintained.
* The system must indicate the patient’s current status: awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2. Visitors
* Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
* The type of visitor must be recorded (e.g., technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
* Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
* Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
* Not all patients are allowed to have an accompanying person. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant people; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when deemed necessary by a physician or social services.
* The system must record the visitor’s exit (mandatory).
* Access outside visiting hours must be blocked unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3. Employees
* Employees must be clearly identified.
* A researcher can only be a nurse or a physician (i.e., researcher is not an independent occupational category separate from clinical roles).
* Still, any employee may be treated by the hospital, act as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4. Addresses
* A person may have more than one registered address.
* Employees periodically update their addresses.
* When possible, patients and visitors should also have their addresses updated.
* It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
* If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should issue an alert requesting a registration update.
2.5. Identification Cards
* Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
* Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
* When a card is in use it is marked active.
* Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card’s identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person’s record while in use.
* Only personalized cards (employees) include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
The hospital units require an integrated time & attendance system covering all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or being assigned impractical schedules.
* Professionals follow different work routines.
* Administration must precisely track the hours worked by each employee.
* Different hospitals in the network may maintain different scheduling contracts with the same employee.
* It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duty, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1. Time & Attendance Records
* The system must record each employee’s clock-in and clock-out times.
* As noted, every employee has an identification card, which facilitates time recording.
* Time records should generate alerts if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record (useful for HR).
3.2. Work Shifts
* Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
* Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
* Nurses may follow fixed schedules or rotate through shifts.
* Physicians may have variable schedules and may visit the hospital only to perform specific duties (consultations, monitoring, surgeries) and leave when those tasks are complete.
* The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
* The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance.
3.3. Availability Exceptions
* Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
* Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1. Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. Upon reporting a loss:
* the card is automatically invalidated;
* a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
* every attempted access with the lost card is blocked and logged;
* the event generates an alert for security.
A card history (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2. Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record:
   * the person involved;
   * reason for the failure;
   * the read attempt;
   * manual verification performed (security/reception staff responsible).
Manual entry/exit validation using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked as inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

response:
REASONING:
STEP 1 - ENTITIES:
ENTITIES: [
  Hospital,
  DepartmentType,
  HospitalDepartment,
  Person,
  PersonRole,
  Employee,
  PhysicianContract,
  ResidentSupervision,
  Patient,
  PatientMovement,
  Visitor,
  VisitorAccess,
  VisitorType,
  CompanionAuthorization,
  Address,
  PersonAddress,
  IdentificationCard,
  CardAssignment,
  CardStatusHistory,
  CardFailureEvent,
  ManualAccessValidation,
  ShiftType,
  WorkSchedule,
  TimeRecord,
  AvailabilityExceptionType,
  AvailabilityException
]

STEP 2 - ATTRIBUTES:
ENTITY [Hospital]:
  * hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  code: varchar(50) (UNIQUE)
  cnpj: varchar(20) (UNIQUE)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  address_line: varchar(255)
  phone: varchar(30)
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [DepartmentType]:
  * department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [HospitalDepartment]:
  * hospital_department_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  name: varchar(150) (NOT NULL)
  capacity_total: int() (NOT NULL)
  capacity_operational: int()
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [Person]:
  * person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  full_name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  cpf: varchar(14) (UNIQUE)
  rg: varchar(20)
  date_of_birth: date() (NOT NULL)
  primary_phone: varchar(30)
  secondary_phone: varchar(30)
  email: varchar(150)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  updated_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PersonRole]:
  * person_role_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  role_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- values: 'EMPLOYEE','PATIENT','VISITOR','PHYSICIAN','NURSE','RESIDENT','RESEARCHER','ADMIN'
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)
  valid_from: date() (NOT NULL)
  valid_to: date()

ENTITY [Employee]:
  * employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  employee_code: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  hire_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  termination_date: date()
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)
  base_hospital_id: int()
  job_category: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- e.g., 'PHYSICIAN','NURSE','ADMIN','OTHER'
  is_researcher: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  supervisor_employee_id: int()

ENTITY [PhysicianContract]:
  * physician_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- e.g., 'CLT','PJ','ON_CALL','DAILY'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [ResidentSupervision]:
  * resident_supervision_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +resident_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +supervising_physician_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()

ENTITY [Patient]:
  * patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  vip_flag: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  medical_record_number: varchar(50) (UNIQUE)
  current_status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'AWAITING','IN_CARE','ADMITTED','OBSERVATION','SURGERY','DISCHARGED'
  wristband_id: varchar(50) (UNIQUE)
  primary_hospital_id: int()
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PatientMovement]:
  * patient_movement_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +from_hospital_department_id: int()
  +to_hospital_department_id: int()
  movement_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ADMISSION','TRANSFER','DISCHARGE'
  movement_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  responsible_employee_id: int()
  notes: varchar(500)

ENTITY [VisitorType]:
  * visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE) -- e.g., 'COMPANION','TECHNICAL','REGULATORY','OTHER'
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [Visitor]:
  * visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  default_visitor_type_id: int()
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [VisitorAccess]:
  * visitor_access_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_department_id: int()
  +visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +identification_card_id: int()
  +accompanied_patient_id: int()
  entry_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  exit_time: datetime()
  access_status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ACTIVE','COMPLETED','BLOCKED','DENIED'
  is_outside_visiting_hours: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  special_authorization_employee_id: int()
  special_authorization_reason: varchar(255)

ENTITY [CompanionAuthorization]:
  * companion_authorization_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  reason_category: varchar(50) (NOT NULL) -- 'MINOR','ELDERLY','PREGNANT','LABOR','POSTPARTUM','PROLONGED','SEVERE','VULNERABILITY','OTHER'
  authorized_by_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  authorized_by_role: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'PHYSICIAN','SOCIAL_SERVICE','OTHER'
  start_datetime: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  end_datetime: datetime()
  notes: varchar(500)

ENTITY [Address]:
  * address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  street: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  number: varchar(20)
  complement: varchar(100)
  neighborhood: varchar(100)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  postal_code: varchar(15)
  country: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Brazil')

ENTITY [PersonAddress]:
  * person_address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +address_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  is_primary: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  last_update_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  source: varchar(30) -- 'EMPLOYEE','PATIENT','VISITOR'
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [IdentificationCard]:
  * identification_card_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT','TEMPORARY'
  access_level: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- maps to color/access zone
  issue_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  expiry_date: date()
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)
  is_personalized: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  photo_url: varchar(255)
  status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ACTIVE','LOST','REPLACED','EXPIRED','INOPERABLE'
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [CardAssignment]:
  * card_assignment_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +identification_card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  assignment_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'EMPLOYEE','TEMP_USE'
  start_datetime: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  end_datetime: datetime()
  is_current: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [CardStatusHistory]:
  * card_status_history_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +identification_card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  old_status: varchar(20)
  new_status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)
  change_datetime: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  reason: varchar(255)
  triggered_by_person_id: int()

ENTITY [CardFailureEvent]:
  * card_failure_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +identification_card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  attempt_datetime: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  failure_reason: varchar(255) (NOT NULL)
  reader_location: varchar(150)
  manual_verification_done: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  manual_verifier_employee_id: int()
  notes: varchar(500)

ENTITY [ManualAccessValidation]:
  * manual_access_validation_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_department_id: int()
  validation_datetime: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  direction: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- 'ENTRY','EXIT'
  official_document_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)
  official_document_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)
  employee_registration_code: varchar(50)
  validated_by_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  legal_equivalence_flag: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)
  related_card_id: int()
  notes: varchar(500)

ENTITY [ShiftType]:
  * shift_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE) -- 'FIXED','ROTATING','VARIABLE'
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [WorkSchedule]:
  * work_schedule_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +shift_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  weekday: int() (NOT NULL) -- 0-6 or 1-7
  start_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  end_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  valid_from: date() (NOT NULL)
  valid_to: date()
  is_active: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1)

ENTITY [TimeRecord]:
  * time_record_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +identification_card_id: int()
  clock_in_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  clock_out_time: datetime()
  source: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'CARD','MANUAL'
  is_missing_clock_alert: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  is_overtime_alert: boolean() (NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [AvailabilityExceptionType]:
  * availability_exception_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE) -- 'DAY_OFF','MEDICAL_LEAVE','VACATION','TRAINING'
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [AvailabilityException]:
  * availability_exception_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +availability_exception_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  note: varchar(500)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  created_by_employee_id: int()

STEP 3 - RELATIONSHIPS:
RELATIONSHIPS:
  - HospitalDepartment relates to Hospital (each department belongs to a specific hospital unit and capacity is per hospital).
  - HospitalDepartment relates to DepartmentType (standard set of department categories across the network).
  - PersonRole relates to Person (roles like patient, visitor, employee etc. are attached to people).
  - Employee relates to Person (employees are people; 1-1 mapping).
  - Employee (supervisor_employee_id) self-relates to Employee (for organizational hierarchy where needed).
  - PhysicianContract relates to Employee and Hospital (physicians can have different contracts at different hospitals).
  - ResidentSupervision relates resident Employee to supervising physician Employee (residents must have supervising physicians).
  - Patient relates to Person (patients are people; 1-1 mapping).
  - PatientMovement relates to Patient (tracks history of movements per patient).
  - PatientMovement relates to HospitalDepartment (from and to departments for admission, transfer, discharge).
  - PatientMovement relates to Employee (responsible staff for the movement).
  - Visitor relates to Person (visitors are people; 1-1 mapping).
  - Visitor relates to VisitorType (default classification).
  - VisitorAccess relates to Visitor (each access event is for a visitor).
  - VisitorAccess relates to Hospital (entry is to a specific hospital).
  - VisitorAccess relates to HospitalDepartment (for department-level access monitoring).
  - VisitorAccess relates to VisitorType (classification per access event, used for restrictions).
  - VisitorAccess relates to IdentificationCard (card used for that visit).
  - VisitorAccess relates to Patient (when visitor is a companion).
  - VisitorAccess relates to Employee (special authorization outside visiting hours).
  - CompanionAuthorization relates to Patient, Visitor and Hospital (who can accompany which patient, where).
  - CompanionAuthorization relates to Employee (who authorized the companion).
  - Address is related to Person via PersonAddress (people can have multiple addresses, with update timestamps).
  - PersonAddress relates to Person and Address (junction for many-to-many and history of updates).
  - IdentificationCard relates to Person via CardAssignment (cards can be assigned to people, temporarily or permanently).
  - CardAssignment relates to IdentificationCard and Person (who uses what card and when).
  - CardStatusHistory relates to IdentificationCard (history of card state: active, lost, replaced, expired).
  - CardStatusHistory relates to Person (who triggered the status change, e.g., reporting a loss).
  - CardFailureEvent relates to IdentificationCard and Person (card failure attempts are tied to a card and a person).
  - CardFailureEvent relates to Employee (manual verifier responsible for manual check).
  - ManualAccessValidation relates to Person (whose entry/exit was validated).
  - ManualAccessValidation relates to Hospital and HospitalDepartment (where the manual access occurred).
  - ManualAccessValidation relates to Employee (who performed the validation).
  - ManualAccessValidation relates to IdentificationCard (optional link to the problematic card).
  - ShiftType relates to WorkSchedule (schedule entries refer to shift type).
  - WorkSchedule relates to Employee (schedule is per employee).
  - WorkSchedule relates to Hospital (same employee can have schedules in different hospitals).
  - TimeRecord relates to Employee (time & attendance per employee).
  - TimeRecord relates to Hospital (to avoid double-scheduling across units).
  - TimeRecord relates to IdentificationCard (card used for clocking in/out).
  - AvailabilityExceptionType relates to AvailabilityException (exception entries refer to a type).
  - AvailabilityException relates to Employee (removes employee from duty).
  - AvailabilityException relates to Employee (created_by_employee_id: who registered the exception).

STEP 4 - CARDINALITIES & FK:
CARDINALITY & FK:
  Relationship 1: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* HospitalDepartment:hospital_id
  Relationship 2: DepartmentType:department_type_id 1--* HospitalDepartment:department_type_id
  Relationship 3: Person:person_id 1--* PersonRole:person_id
  Relationship 4: Person:person_id 1--1 Employee:person_id
  Relationship 5: Employee:employee_id 1--* Employee:supervisor_employee_id
  Relationship 6: Employee:employee_id 1--* PhysicianContract:employee_id
  Relationship 7: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* PhysicianContract:hospital_id
  Relationship 8: Employee:employee_id 1--* ResidentSupervision:resident_employee_id
  Relationship 9: Employee:employee_id 1--* ResidentSupervision:supervising_physician_employee_id
  Relationship10: Person:person_id 1--1 Patient:person_id
  Relationship11: Patient:patient_id 1--* PatientMovement:patient_id
  Relationship12: HospitalDepartment:hospital_department_id 1--* PatientMovement:from_hospital_department_id
  Relationship13: HospitalDepartment:hospital_department_id 1--* PatientMovement:to_hospital_department_id
  Relationship14: Employee:employee_id 1--* PatientMovement:responsible_employee_id
  Relationship15: Person:person_id 1--1 Visitor:person_id
  Relationship16: VisitorType:visitor_type_id 1--* Visitor:default_visitor_type_id
  Relationship17: Visitor:visitor_id 1--* VisitorAccess:visitor_id
  Relationship18: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* VisitorAccess:hospital_id
  Relationship19: HospitalDepartment:hospital_department_id 1--* VisitorAccess:hospital_department_id
  Relationship20: VisitorType:visitor_type_id 1--* VisitorAccess:visitor_type_id
  Relationship21: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* VisitorAccess:identification_card_id
  Relationship22: Patient:patient_id 1--* VisitorAccess:accompanied_patient_id
  Relationship23: Employee:employee_id 1--* VisitorAccess:special_authorization_employee_id
  Relationship24: Patient:patient_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:patient_id
  Relationship25: Visitor:visitor_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:visitor_id
  Relationship26: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:hospital_id
  Relationship27: Employee:employee_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:authorized_by_employee_id
  Relationship28: Person:person_id 1--* PersonAddress:person_id
  Relationship29: Address:address_id 1--* PersonAddress:address_id
  Relationship30: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardAssignment:identification_card_id
  Relationship31: Person:person_id 1--* CardAssignment:person_id
  Relationship32: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:identification_card_id
  Relationship33: Person:person_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:triggered_by_person_id
  Relationship34: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:identification_card_id
  Relationship35: Person:person_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:person_id
  Relationship36: Employee:employee_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:manual_verifier_employee_id
  Relationship37: Person:person_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:person_id
  Relationship38: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:hospital_id
  Relationship39: HospitalDepartment:hospital_department_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:hospital_department_id
  Relationship40: Employee:employee_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:validated_by_employee_id
  Relationship41: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:related_card_id
  Relationship42: ShiftType:shift_type_id 1--* WorkSchedule:shift_type_id
  Relationship43: Employee:employee_id 1--* WorkSchedule:employee_id
  Relationship44: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* WorkSchedule:hospital_id
  Relationship45: Employee:employee_id 1--* TimeRecord:employee_id
  Relationship46: Hospital:hospital_id 1--* TimeRecord:hospital_id
  Relationship47: IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* TimeRecord:identification_card_id
  Relationship48: AvailabilityExceptionType:availability_exception_type_id 1--* AvailabilityException:availability_exception_type_id
  Relationship49: Employee:employee_id 1--* AvailabilityException:employee_id
  Relationship50: Employee:employee_id 1--* AvailabilityException:created_by_employee_id

STEP 5 - VALIDATION:
VALIDATION:
  ✓ All primary keys defined and unique
  ✓ All foreign keys reference existing tables and columns
  ✓ Cardinalities reflect the domain:
      - Hospitals host multiple departments; departments are per hospital.
      - People can hold multiple roles; each employee/patient/visitor is tied to exactly one person.
      - Physicians can have multiple contracts across hospitals.
      - Residents supervised by physicians with date ranges.
      - Patients have full movement history across departments.
      - Visitors have multiple access events with classification and optional companion link.
      - Companion authorization is specific to patient, visitor and hospital with authorized staff.
      - People can have multiple addresses with update timestamps.
      - Cards can be reused and reassigned; history of status and failures is preserved.
      - Time & attendance supports multiple hospitals and detailed schedules per employee.
      - Availability exceptions are typed and documented.
  ✓ Many-to-many relationships handled with junction tables:
      - Person–Address via PersonAddress.
      - Person–IdentificationCard via CardAssignment.
      - Employee–Hospital (for physicians) via PhysicianContract and also via WorkSchedule for scheduling.
  ✓ No orphaned tables or attributes; all derived from explicit requirements.
  No issues found

JSON OUTPUT:
{
  "tables": {
    "Hospital": {

    "Employee:employee_id 1--* VisitorAccess:special_authorization_employee_id",
    "Patient:patient_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:patient_id",
    "Visitor:visitor_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:visitor_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:hospital_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:authorized_by_employee_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* PersonAddress:person_id",
    "Address:address_id 1--* PersonAddress:address_id",
    "IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardAssignment:identification_card_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* CardAssignment:person_id",
    "IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:identification_card_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:triggered_by_person_id",
    "IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:identification_card_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:person_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:manual_verifier_employee_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:person_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:hospital_id",
    "HospitalDepartment:hospital_department_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:hospital_department_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:validated_by_employee_id",
    "IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* ManualAccessValidation:related_card_id",
    "ShiftType:shift_type_id 1--* WorkSchedule:shift_type_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* WorkSchedule:employee_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* WorkSchedule:hospital_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* TimeRecord:employee_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* TimeRecord:hospital_id",
    "IdentificationCard:identification_card_id 1--* TimeRecord:identification_card_id",
    "AvailabilityExceptionType:availability_exception_type_id 1--* AvailabilityException:availability_exception_type_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* AvailabilityException:employee_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* AvailabilityException:created_by_employee_id"
  ],

  "label": "Integrated Hospital Network Access, Patients, Visitors and Time & Attendance ER Model"
}

formatted_human_prompt:
Analyze the following database requirement. You MUST follow the 5-step reasoning process 
defined in the system prompt. Show your complete thinking for each step, then output the JSON.

Database requirement:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor everyone who enters and leaves the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution’s internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital contains departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network must offer every service: each hospital declares which departments, human resources and capacities it provides (available teams and departments).
Departments in the network include:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1. Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader/responsible person for every shift (i.e., there is always someone accountable for the department at any time).
2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals from multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. A single person may assume more than one role (for example, a physician may be admitted as a patient outside working hours).
* Physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types (e.g., CLT: permanent employment, PJ: contracted as a legal entity, on-call/shift, daily rate).
* Residents are licensed physicians in training who attend to patients under supervision; it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident.
* VIP patients have special rules for care and enhanced privacy (these rules relate to privacy and procedures, not to the medical quality of care).
* Employees may have dependents who are also patients.
* The hospital records the following for every person who accesses its facilities: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF = Brazilian taxpayer ID; RG = identity card.)
2.1. Patients
* Each patient receives an identification wristband.
* The system must record patient movements between departments, including admission, inter-department transfers and discharge.
* A complete history of the departments visited by each patient must be maintained.
* The system must indicate the patient’s current status: awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2. Visitors
* Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
* The type of visitor must be recorded (e.g., technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
* Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
* Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
* Not all patients are allowed to have an accompanying person. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant people; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when deemed necessary by a physician or social services.
* The system must record the visitor’s exit (mandatory).
* Access outside visiting hours must be blocked unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3. Employees
* Employees must be clearly identified.
* A researcher can only be a nurse or a physician (i.e., researcher is not an independent occupational category separate from clinical roles).
* Still, any employee may be treated by the hospital, act as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4. Addresses
* A person may have more than one registered address.
* Employees periodically update their addresses.
* When possible, patients and visitors should also have their addresses updated.
* It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
* If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should issue an alert requesting a registration update.
2.5. Identification Cards
* Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
* Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
* When a card is in use it is marked active.
* Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card’s identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person’s record while in use.
* Only personalized cards (employees) include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
The hospital units require an integrated time & attendance system covering all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or being assigned impractical schedules.
* Professionals follow different work routines.
* Administration must precisely track the hours worked by each employee.
* Different hospitals in the network may maintain different scheduling contracts with the same employee.
* It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duty, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1. Time & Attendance Records
* The system must record each employee’s clock-in and clock-out times.
* As noted, every employee has an identification card, which facilitates time recording.
* Time records should generate alerts if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record (useful for HR).
3.2. Work Shifts
* Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
* Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
* Nurses may follow fixed schedules or rotate through shifts.
* Physicians may have variable schedules and may visit the hospital only to perform specific duties (consultations, monitoring, surgeries) and leave when those tasks are complete.
* The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
* The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance.
3.3. Availability Exceptions
* Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
* Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1. Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. Upon reporting a loss:
* the card is automatically invalidated;
* a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
* every attempted access with the lost card is blocked and logged;
* the event generates an alert for security.
A card history (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2. Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record:
   * the person involved;
   * reason for the failure;
   * the read attempt;
   * manual verification performed (security/reception staff responsible).
Manual entry/exit validation using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked as inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

Remember:
1. Complete STEP 1 - STEP 5 fully before generating JSON
2. Be explicit and thorough in your reasoning
3. Reference the requirement directly when explaining your choices
4. Output ONLY the JSON after your reasoning (no additional commentary)

response:
REASONING:
STEP 1 - ENTITIES:
ENTITIES: [
  Hospital,
  DepartmentType,
  Department,
  Shift,
  Person,
  PersonRole,
  Employee,
  PhysicianContract,
  Residency,
  Patient,
  PatientStatusHistory,
  PatientMovement,
  Visitor,
  VisitorType,
  CompanionAuthorization,
  EmployeeDependent,
  Address,
  PersonAddress,
  IdentificationCard,
  CardStatusHistory,
  CardAccessAttempt,
  CardFailureEvent,
  AccessEvent,
  WorkContract,
  WorkShift,
  EmployeeSchedule,
  TimeRecord,
  AvailabilityException,
  CapacityAlertThreshold
]

Explanation:

- Hospital: each facility in the network.
- DepartmentType: catalog of possible departments (Emergency, ICU, etc.).
- Department: concrete department instance within a hospital, with capacity and coordinator.
- Shift: time ranges used to define “every shift has a leader”.
- Person: everyone who accesses facilities (staff, visitors, patients); holds core identity data.
- PersonRole: because a person may assume more than one role (visitor, patient, physician, etc.), we store roles separately.
- Employee: subset of persons who are employees (physician, nurse, resident, administrative staff, researcher flag).
- PhysicianContract: physicians may work at multiple units under different contract types; we need per-hospital, per-physician contract data.
- Residency: residents are physicians in training; we must record supervising physician per resident.
- Patient: subset of persons who are patients; includes VIP flag and wristband.
- PatientStatusHistory: to store current and historical status values (awaiting care, in care, admitted, etc.).
- PatientMovement: to record patient movements between departments, admission, transfers, discharge, and maintain a complete history of departments visited.
- Visitor: subset of persons who are visitors; includes card and classification.
- VisitorType: catalog of visitor types (technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
- CompanionAuthorization: to record when a visitor is an accompanying person of a patient, with reason category and authorization info.
- EmployeeDependent: employees may have dependents who are also patients; we model employee–dependent relationships.
- Address: address entity.
- PersonAddress: because a person may have more than one registered address, and we must store timestamp of last update.
- IdentificationCard: cards with issue/expiry, type (access level), active flag, photo for personalized cards; linked to person when in use.
- CardStatusHistory: card history (active, replaced, lost, expired).
- CardAccessAttempt: to log every attempted access, especially for lost cards (blocked and logged).
- CardFailureEvent: for card failure (defective or inoperable) including person, reason, read attempt, manual verification staff.
- AccessEvent: general access history (entry/exit) by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
- WorkContract: per-employee, per-hospital employment contract (CLT, PJ, etc.).
- WorkShift: generic shift patterns (start/end times, type).
- EmployeeSchedule: assignment of shifts to employees per contract; prevents double-booking across units.
- TimeRecord: clock-in/out times, linked to employee and card.
- AvailabilityException: events that remove a professional from duty (day off, medical leave, vacation, training).
- CapacityAlertThreshold: to store thresholds (60%, 80%, 100%) per department or globally for generating capacity alerts.

STEP 2 - ATTRIBUTES:
ENTITY [Hospital]:
  * hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  code: varchar(20) (UNIQUE)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  address_text: varchar(255) (NOT NULL)
  phone: varchar(30)
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [DepartmentType]:
  * department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [Department]:
  * department_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +department_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  name: varchar(150) (NOT NULL)
  capacity_total: int() (NOT NULL)
  capacity_operational: int() (NOT NULL)
  +coordinator_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Shift]:
  * shift_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  start_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  end_time: time() (NOT NULL)
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [Person]:
  * person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  full_name: varchar(200) (NOT NULL)
  cpf: varchar(14) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  rg: varchar(20) (NOT NULL)
  date_of_birth: date() (NOT NULL)
  primary_phone: varchar(30)
  secondary_phone: varchar(30)
  email: varchar(150)
  created_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  updated_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PersonRole]:
  * person_role_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  role_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- ENUM-like: 'VISITOR','PATIENT','PHYSICIAN','NURSE','RESIDENT','RESEARCHER','ADMIN'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Employee]:
  * employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  employee_code: varchar(30) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  hire_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  termination_date: date()
  job_category: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'PHYSICIAN','NURSE','RESIDENT','ADMIN'
  is_researcher: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [PhysicianContract]:
  * physician_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'CLT','PJ','ON_CALL','DAILY_RATE'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()
  workload_hours_per_week: decimal(5,2)
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Residency]:
  * residency_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +resident_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +supervisor_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  specialty: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  program_start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  program_end_date: date()
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [Patient]:
  * patient_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  wristband_code: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  is_vip: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  registration_date: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  current_status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'AWAITING','IN_CARE','ADMITTED','OBSERVATION','SURGERY','DISCHARGED'

ENTITY [PatientStatusHistory]:
  * patient_status_history_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  status: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)
  status_start: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  status_end: datetime()
  reason: varchar(255)

ENTITY [PatientMovement]:
  * patient_movement_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +from_department_id: int()
  +to_department_id: int()
  movement_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ADMISSION','TRANSFER','DISCHARGE'
  movement_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [VisitorType]:
  * visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [Visitor]:
  * visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +visitor_type_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  first_visit_date: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  last_visit_date: datetime()
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [CompanionAuthorization]:
  * companion_authorization_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +visitor_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  reason_category: varchar(50) (NOT NULL) -- 'MINOR','ELDERLY','PREGNANT','LABOR','POSTPARTUM','PROLONGED','SEVERE','VULNERABILITY'
  authorized_by_role: varchar(50) (NOT NULL) -- 'PHYSICIAN','SOCIAL_SERVICE'
  +authorized_by_employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  authorization_start: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  authorization_end: datetime()
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [EmployeeDependent]:
  * employee_dependent_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +patient_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  relationship: varchar(50) (NOT NULL) -- 'SPOUSE','CHILD','PARENT','OTHER'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()

ENTITY [Address]:
  * address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  street: varchar(150) (NOT NULL)
  number: varchar(20)
  complement: varchar(100)
  neighborhood: varchar(100)
  city: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  state: varchar(2) (NOT NULL)
  postal_code: varchar(15)
  country: varchar(50) (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [PersonAddress]:
  * person_address_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +address_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  is_primary: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  last_update_at: datetime() (NOT NULL)

ENTITY [IdentificationCard]:
  * card_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_number: varchar(50) (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  card_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT','TEMPORARY'
  access_level: varchar(30) (NOT NULL)
  issue_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  expiry_date: date()
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)
  personalized_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  photo_url: varchar(255)
  +current_person_id: int()
  status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ACTIVE','LOST','REPLACED','EXPIRED','INOPERABLE'

ENTITY [CardStatusHistory]:
  * card_status_history_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  status: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ACTIVE','LOST','REPLACED','EXPIRED','INOPERABLE'
  status_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  +changed_by_employee_id: int()
  notes: varchar(255)

ENTITY [CardAccessAttempt]:
  * card_access_attempt_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  attempt_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  +department_id: int()
  result: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'ALLOWED','BLOCKED'
  reason: varchar(100) (NOT NULL) -- e.g. 'LOST_CARD','OUT_OF_HOURS','NO_AUTH'
  +person_id: int()
  direction: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- 'ENTRY','EXIT'

ENTITY [CardFailureEvent]:
  * card_failure_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +card_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  failure_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  failure_reason: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  read_attempt_description: varchar(255)
  manual_verification_performed: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  +verified_by_employee_id: int()
  manual_entry_recorded: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  +related_access_event_id: int()

ENTITY [AccessEvent]:
  * access_event_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +person_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +card_id: int()
  +department_id: int()
  hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  access_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  direction: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- 'ENTRY','EXIT'
  person_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'EMPLOYEE','VISITOR','PATIENT'
  validated_manually: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  +validated_by_employee_id: int()
  source: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'CARD_READER','MANUAL'

ENTITY [WorkContract]:
  * work_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +hospital_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  contract_type: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'CLT','PJ','ON_CALL','DAILY_RATE','OTHER'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date()
  standard_hours_per_week: decimal(5,2)
  active_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [WorkShift]:
  * work_shift_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  name: varchar(100) (NOT NULL)
  shift_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'FIXED','ROTATING','VARIABLE'
  start_time: time()
  end_time: time()
  description: varchar(255)

ENTITY [EmployeeSchedule]:
  * employee_schedule_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +work_contract_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +work_shift_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  weekday: int() (NOT NULL) -- 0-6
  start_datetime: datetime()
  end_datetime: datetime()
  +department_id: int()
  is_active: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

ENTITY [TimeRecord]:
  * time_record_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  +card_id: int()
  record_time: datetime() (NOT NULL)
  record_type: varchar(10) (NOT NULL) -- 'IN','OUT'
  source: varchar(20) (NOT NULL) -- 'CARD_READER','MANUAL'
  +validated_by_employee_id: int()
  anomaly_flag: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT FALSE)
  anomaly_type: varchar(50) -- 'MISSING_CLOCK','EXCEEDED_HOURS'

ENTITY [AvailabilityException]:
  * availability_exception_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +employee_id: int() (NOT NULL)
  exception_type: varchar(30) (NOT NULL) -- 'DAY_OFF','MEDICAL_LEAVE','VACATION','TRAINING'
  start_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  end_date: date() (NOT NULL)
  note: varchar(255)

ENTITY [CapacityAlertThreshold]:
  * capacity_alert_threshold_id: int() (NOT NULL, UNIQUE)
  +department_id: int()
  threshold_percent: decimal(5,2) (NOT NULL) -- e.g. 60.00, 80.00, 100.00
  alert_enabled: boolean (NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE)

STEP 3 - RELATIONSHIPS:
RELATIONSHIPS:
  - Hospital relates to Department (each department belongs to one hospital; hospitals declare which departments they provide).
  - DepartmentType relates to Department (departments are instances of catalogued types like Emergency, ICU).
  - Department relates to Employee (each department has a coordinator employee).
  - Shift relates to Department and Employee (each department must have a leader per shift; implicit relationship for leadership assignment; modeled via EmployeeSchedule/WorkShift and coordinator, but no separate table).
  - Person relates to PersonRole (a person may assume more than one role).
  - Person relates to Employee (an employee is a person; one-to-one).
  - Employee relates to PhysicianContract (physician may work at multiple units under different contracts; only physicians should have PhysicianContract, but enforced by job_category).
  - Employee relates to Residency (residents are employees; residency links resident to supervising physician employee).
  - Person relates to Patient (patient is a person; one-to-one).
  - Patient relates to PatientStatusHistory (complete history of patient status).
  - Patient relates to PatientMovement (complete history of departments visited; movements).
  - Department relates to PatientMovement (movements between departments).
  - Person relates to Visitor (visitor is a person; one-to-one).
  - VisitorType relates to Visitor (visitor classification for access restrictions).
  - Patient relates to Visitor via CompanionAuthorization (some visitors accompany patients; not all visitors are companions).
  - Employee relates to CompanionAuthorization (authorization may be granted by a physician or social services).
  - Employee relates to EmployeeDependent and Patient relates to EmployeeDependent (employees may have dependents who are also patients).
  - Person relates to PersonAddress and Address (a person may have more than one registered address; timestamped).
  - IdentificationCard relates to Person (card may be temporarily linked to person; employees have personalized cards).
  - IdentificationCard relates to CardStatusHistory (card history: active, replaced, lost, expired, inoperable).
  - IdentificationCard relates to CardAccessAttempt (every attempted access with the card, especially blocked attempts for lost cards).
  - IdentificationCard, Person, Employee relate to CardFailureEvent (card failure event includes person; manual verification staff; links optionally to AccessEvent).
  - Person, IdentificationCard, Department, Hospital relate to AccessEvent (access history by person, date, department and type).
  - Employee, Hospital relate to WorkContract (different hospitals may maintain different scheduling contracts with same employee).
  - WorkContract relates to WorkShift via EmployeeSchedule (employees scheduled to shifts, possibly with department).
  - EmployeeSchedule relates to Department (shifts often tied to a department).
  - Employee relates to TimeRecord (time & attendance; clock-in/out).
  - TimeRecord relates to IdentificationCard (employee uses card for clocking).
  - Employee relates to AvailabilityException (records that remove employee from duty).
  - Department relates to CapacityAlertThreshold (capacity alert thresholds per department).

STEP 5 - VALIDATION:
VALIDATION:
  ✓ All primary keys defined and unique.
  ✓ All foreign keys reference existing entities and appropriate columns.
  ✓ Cardinalities are logical for the described domain:
      - Persons can have multiple roles, addresses, and cards over time.
      - Employees can have multiple contracts across hospitals and multiple schedules.
      - Patients, visitors and employees are all derived from Person with one-to-one specialization tables.
      - Many-to-many relationships (e.g., patient–visitor companions, employee–patient dependents) are resolved via junction tables (CompanionAuthorization, EmployeeDependent).
  ✓ Many-to-many handled with junction tables where needed.
  ✓ No orphaned tables or attributes; each table serves a stated requirement:
      - Movement, status history, and access events enable traceability and occupancy reporting.
      - TimeRecord, WorkContract, EmployeeSchedule, AvailabilityException support scheduling and HR compliance.
      - CardStatusHistory, CardAccessAttempt, CardFailureEvent support security, lost/defective card procedures.
      - CapacityAlertThreshold supports 60/80/100% capacity alerts.
  No issues found.

    },
    "PersonAddress": {
      "*person_address_id": "int()",
      "+person_id": "int()",
      "+address_id": "int()",
      "is_primary": "boolean",
      "last_update_at": "datetime()"
    },
    "IdentificationCard": {
      "*card_id": "int()",
      "card_number": "varchar(50)",
      "card_type": "varchar(30)",
      "access_level": "varchar(30)",
      "issue_date": "date()",
      "expiry_date": "date()",
      "active_flag": "boolean",
      "personalized_flag": "boolean",
      "photo_url": "varchar(255)",
      "+current_person_id": "int()",
      "status": "varchar(20)"
    },
    "CardStatusHistory": {
      "*card_status_history_id": "int()",
      "+card_id": "int()",
      "+changed_by_employee_id": "int()",
      "status": "varchar(20)",
      "status_time": "datetime()",
      "notes": "varchar(255)"
    },
    "CardAccessAttempt": {
      "*card_access_attempt_id": "int()",
      "+card_id": "int()",
      "+department_id": "int()",
      "+person_id": "int()",
      "attempt_time": "datetime()",
      "result": "varchar(20)",
      "reason": "varchar(100)",
      "direction": "varchar(10)"
    },
    "CardFailureEvent": {
      "*card_failure_event_id": "int()",
      "+card_id": "int()",
      "+person_id": "int()",
      "+verified_by_employee_id": "int()",
      "+related_access_event_id": "int()",
      "failure_time": "datetime()",
      "failure_reason": "varchar(100)",
      "read_attempt_description": "varchar(255)",
      "manual_verification_performed": "boolean",
      "manual_entry_recorded": "boolean"
    },
    "AccessEvent": {
      "*access_event_id": "int()",
      "+person_id": "int()",
      "+card_id": "int()",
      "+department_id": "int()",
      "hospital_id": "int()",
      "+validated_by_employee_id": "int()",
      "access_time": "datetime()",
      "direction": "varchar(10)",
      "person_type": "varchar(20)",
      "validated_manually": "boolean",
      "source": "varchar(20)"
    },
    "WorkContract": {
      "*work_contract_id": "int()",
      "+employee_id": "int()",
      "+hospital_id": "int()",
      "contract_type": "varchar(20)",
      "start_date": "date()",
      "end_date": "date()",
      "standard_hours_per_week": "decimal(5,2)",
      "active_flag": "boolean"
    },
    "WorkShift": {
      "*work_shift_id": "int()",
      "name": "varchar(100)",
      "shift_type": "varchar(30)",
      "start_time": "time()",
      "end_time": "time()",
      "description": "varchar(255)"
    },
    "EmployeeSchedule": {
      "*employee_schedule_id": "int()",
      "+work_contract_id": "int()",
      "+work_shift_id": "int()",
      "+department_id": "int()",
      "weekday": "int()",
      "start_datetime": "datetime()",
      "end_datetime": "datetime()",
      "is_active": "boolean"
    },
    "TimeRecord": {
      "*time_record_id": "int()",
      "+employee_id": "int()",
      "+card_id": "int()",
      "+validated_by_employee_id": "int()",
      "record_time": "datetime()",
      "record_type": "varchar(10)",
      "source": "varchar(20)",
      "anomaly_flag": "boolean",
      "anomaly_type": "varchar(50)"
    },
    "AvailabilityException": {
      "*availability_exception_id": "int()",
      "+employee_id": "int()",
      "exception_type": "varchar(30)",
      "start_date": "date()",
      "end_date": "date()",
      "note": "varchar(255)"
    },
    "CapacityAlertThreshold": {
      "*capacity_alert_threshold_id": "int()",
      "+department_id": "int()",
      "threshold_percent": "decimal(5,2)",
      "alert_enabled": "boolean"
    }
  },
  "relations": [
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* Department:hospital_id",
    "DepartmentType:department_type_id 1--* Department:department_type_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* Department:coordinator_employee_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* PersonRole:person_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--1 Employee:person_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* PhysicianContract:employee_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* PhysicianContract:hospital_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--1 Residency:resident_employee_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* Residency:supervisor_employee_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--1 Patient:person_id",
    "Patient:patient_id 1--* PatientStatusHistory:patient_id",
    "Patient:patient_id 1--* PatientMovement:patient_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* PatientMovement:from_department_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* PatientMovement:to_department_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--1 Visitor:person_id",
    "VisitorType:visitor_type_id 1--* Visitor:visitor_type_id",
    "Patient:patient_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:patient_id",
    "Visitor:visitor_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:visitor_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* CompanionAuthorization:authorized_by_employee_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* EmployeeDependent:employee_id",
    "Patient:patient_id 1--* EmployeeDependent:patient_id",
    "Address:address_id 1--* PersonAddress:address_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* PersonAddress:person_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* IdentificationCard:current_person_id",
    "IdentificationCard:card_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:card_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* CardStatusHistory:changed_by_employee_id",
    "IdentificationCard:card_id 1--* CardAccessAttempt:card_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* CardAccessAttempt:department_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* CardAccessAttempt:person_id",
    "IdentificationCard:card_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:card_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:person_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:verified_by_employee_id",
    "AccessEvent:access_event_id 1--* CardFailureEvent:related_access_event_id",
    "Person:person_id 1--* AccessEvent:person_id",
    "IdentificationCard:card_id 1--* AccessEvent:card_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* AccessEvent:department_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* AccessEvent:hospital_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* AccessEvent:validated_by_employee_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* WorkContract:employee_id",
    "Hospital:hospital_id 1--* WorkContract:hospital_id",
    "WorkContract:work_contract_id 1--* EmployeeSchedule:work_contract_id",
    "WorkShift:work_shift_id 1--* EmployeeSchedule:work_shift_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* EmployeeSchedule:department_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* TimeRecord:employee_id",
    "IdentificationCard:card_id 1--* TimeRecord:card_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* TimeRecord:validated_by_employee_id",
    "Employee:employee_id 1--* AvailabilityException:employee_id",
    "Department:department_id 1--* CapacityAlertThreshold:department_id"
  ],
  "rankAdjustments": "",
  "label": "Integrated Hospital Network Access, Patient Flow, and Workforce Management ER Model"
}

formatted_human_prompt:
Analyze the following database requirement. You MUST follow the 5-step reasoning process 
defined in the system prompt. Show your complete thinking for each step, then output the JSON.

Database requirement:
ER Modeling Scenario
Integrated Hospital Management System for Multiple Facilities
Business Context
You have been hired to model the database for a hospital network that operates 15 units across 3 Brazilian states. The system must control, record and monitor the entry and exit of all people who access the hospitals, including staff, visitors and patients, ensuring traceability, security and compliance with the institution's internal rules.
Functional Requirements
1. Hospital
Each hospital has departments responsible for specific activities. Not every facility in the network needs to offer all services: each hospital declares the departments, human resources and capacities it has (available teams and departments).
Departments present in the network:
* Emergency
* ICU (Intensive Care Unit)
* Surgical Center / Operating Theaters
* Adult Ward
* Pediatric Ward
* Maternity
* Radiology / Imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI)
* Clinical Laboratory
* Outpatient Clinic
* Hospital Pharmacy
* Social Services
* Triage
* Reception
1.1 Departments
* Each department has an assigned coordinator.
* There must be a leader or person responsible for the department in every shift (there should always be someone accountable for the department at any time).

2. People Management
The hospital receives individuals in multiple categories; some are employees and others are not. Categories include: visitor, patient, physician, nurse, resident physician, researcher and administrative staff. Physicians may work in multiple units under different contract types (for example, CLT, PJ, on-call, daily-rate). Residents are licensed physicians in training who also attend patients under supervision, so it is important to record the supervising physician for each resident. VIP patients have special care rules and enhanced privacy (these are procedural and privacy measures, not related to the medical quality of care). Employees may have dependents who are also patients. The hospital records the following for every person who accesses it: full name, addresses, contact phone numbers, CPF, RG and date of birth. (CPF and RG are Brazilian identifiers: CPF = taxpayer ID, RG = identity card.)
2.1 Patients
   * Each patient receives an identification wristband.
   * The system must record patient movements between departments, including hospital admission, transfers between departments and discharge.
   * A complete history of the departments each patient has passed through must be maintained.
   * The system must indicate whether the patient is awaiting care, in care, admitted (inpatient), under observation, in surgery, or discharged.
2.2 Visitors
   * Not every visitor is accompanying a patient.
   * The type of visitor must be recorded (for example, technical visit, regulatory inspection, etc.).
   * Visitor classification is essential to determine access restrictions (important information for the security team).
   * Each visitor receives an identification card at entry.
   * Not every patient is allowed to have a companion. Valid companion cases include: minors; elderly patients; pregnant patients; women in labor; postpartum patients; patients with prolonged or severe hospitalization, at the discretion of the attending physician or care team; and situations of emotional or cognitive vulnerability when the physician or social services deem it necessary.
   * If patient isolation is required, the physician must document the reason for isolation in a clinical note.
   * The system must record the visitor's exit (mandatory).
   * Access outside visiting hours must be prevented unless special authorization is granted by the hospital.
2.3 Employees
   * Employees must be clearly identified.
   * A researcher can be either a nurse or a physician only; researcher is not an independent occupational category.
   * A resident physician cannot be a coordinator or be responsible for a department, even temporarily.
   * Physicians and nurses may also hold administrative roles (for example, responsible for coordinating a department).
   * Any employee may still be treated by the hospital, be present as a visitor, or accompany a patient.
2.4 Addresses
   * A person may have more than one registered address.
   * Employees update their addresses periodically.
   * When possible, patients and visitors should also have updated addresses.
   * It is important to store the timestamp of the last address update.
   * If the last update was more than six months ago, the system should alert and request a registration update.
2.5 Identification Cards
   * Identification cards have an issue date and an expiry date, recorded in the system for security purposes.
   * Cards have a type that designates access level (visually distinguished by different colors).
   * When a card is in use it is marked as active.
   * Because patients and visitors do not have personalized cards, a card's identifier number may be temporarily linked to the person's record while in use.
   * Only personalized cards (employees) must include a photo.
3. Employee Scheduling
Hospital units require an integrated time and attendance system that covers all 15 units to prevent staff from being accidentally scheduled at two locations at the same time or assigned impractical schedules. Professionals follow different work routines. Administration must precisely control the hours worked by each employee. Different hospitals in the network may have different scheduling contracts with the same employee. It is also necessary to record events that remove an employee from duties, such as days off, medical leave, vacation or training.
3.1 Time Clock Records
   * The system must record each employee's clock-in and clock-out times.
   * As mentioned, each employee has an identification card, which facilitates this task.
   * Time records may generate an alert if an employee exceeds scheduled hours or misses a clock record; this is useful for HR.
3.2 Work Shifts
   * Some employees have more flexible shifts than others.
   * Administrative staff generally have fixed weekdays and start/end times.
   * Nurses may have fixed rules or work rotating shifts.
   * Physicians similarly may have variable schedules and may come to the hospital only to perform consultations, monitoring, surgeries, etc., leaving when those activities are completed.
   * The employment contract governs how working hours are administered by the contracting institution.
   * The key requirement is accurate recording of clock-in and clock-out times for HR verification of compliance with agreed schedules.
3.3 Availability Exceptions
   * Reasons that remove a professional from scheduled activities must be well documented.
   * Each exception should include start and end dates, type (day off, medical leave, vacation, training) and an optional textual note.
3.4 Shift Swaps
   * Shift swaps among health staff are common and permitted.
   * Whenever a swap occurs, it is important to record the involved employees and the coordinator who approved the swap.
   * Each department has its coordinator, who may be a physician, a nurse or an administrative employee.
   * The justification for the swap request should also be recorded (useful for HR metrics about the need for permanent schedule changes, employee health considerations, etc.).
4. Special Situations and Contingency Procedures
4.1 Lost Identification Card
The system must allow immediate registration of a lost card. When a loss is reported:
   * the card is automatically invalidated;
   * a new card may be issued and linked to the person;
   * every access attempt using the lost card is blocked and logged;
   * the event generates an alert for security.
A history of cards (active, replaced, lost, expired) must be maintained.
4.2 Card Failure (Defective or Inoperable Card)
When a card fails to read, the system must record: the person involved; the reason for the failure; the read attempt; and the manual verification performed (security or reception staff responsible). Manual validation of entry/exit using an official document plus employee ID/registration must be allowed. After manual validation, the system records the entry/exit with the same legal validity as an electronic read. The defective card should be marked inoperable and require replacement.
5. Reports and Queries
      * Query access history by person, date, department and type (employee, visitor, patient).
      * Generate department occupancy reports.
      * Generate reports on compliance with scheduled hours and total hours worked for a given period (month, week, day).
      * The system must alert whenever a department reaches 60%, 80% and 100% of its operational capacity.

Remember:
1. Complete STEP 1 - STEP 5 fully before generating JSON
2. Be explicit and thorough in your reasoning
3. Reference the requirement directly when explaining your choices
4. Output ONLY the JSON after your reasoning (no additional commentary)
